brief_justification: Paper A is an experimental condensed-matter study that identifies a physical decoherence mechanism (Coulomb blockade and microwave-driven charge tunnelling in metallic grains) in superconducting circuits via scanning gate microscopy, whereas Paper B is a Bayesian hierarchical statistical model coupling a log-Gaussian Cox point process with spatial probit regression through a shared Gaussian process to test for preferential sampling in retail survival data. There is no common transferable machinery -- one is charge-transport device physics, the other is spatial point-process inference -- so no role-for-role mapping holds beyond the superficial notion of a hidden factor influencing outcomes.
